Full fidelity provenance populated PESTO’s multi-fidelity pesto_ies_callback() runs.

title: kernR 0.4.0
text: New ksd_test(): one-sample kernel Stein discrepancy goodness--fit test. mmd_test() compares two samples, ksd_test() compares sample distribution supplied score (gradient log density), target may unnormalised reference sample needed. calibration framing direct – given posterior ensemble draws score distribution claim represent, test asks whether draws actually follow . sensitive mean, variance, tail mis-specification, calibrates degenerate U-statistic null wild bootstrap (Chwialkowski et al., 2016). default base kernel inverse multi-quadric (IMQ), detects non-convergence regimes Gaussian kernel blind dimension grows (Gorham & Mackey, 2017); Gaussian (RBF) base kernel remains available via kernel = "rbf". New gaussian_score(): score-function factory multivariate-normal target, use score argument ksd_test(). New concordance_test(): kernel k-sample concordance test asking whether several samples – posterior draws different inference engines, scenario ensembles different simulators – come common distribution. statistic summed pairwise Maximum Mean Discrepancy single joint-permutation null (family-wise error controlled), result carries full pairwise discrepancy matrix, rejection localises source diverges margin. New numeric_score(): finite-difference score adapter turns (possibly unnormalised) log density score ksd_test() needs, target need expressible log-density function, hand-differentiated. New vignette Calibration concordance: kernR’s validation layer tying ksd_test(), concordance_test(), numeric_score() together.

title: kernR 0.3.1

New coverage public density-ratio API weight diagnostics (test-density-ratio-api.R): fit_density_ratio() / predict_density_ratio() round-trip reproducibility, plot_weights(), new print.cme_fit() method.

title: kernR 0.3.0
text: DR-DATE DR-DETT now genuinely doubly robust. dr_date_test() previously fitted conditional mean embedding outcome model discarded , returning inverse-probability-weighted statistic regardless outcome_model – outcome_model = "krr" "zero" gave identical results despite documented double-robustness claim. statistic now forms augmented (AIPW) counterfactual mean embeddings, consistent either propensity outcome model correctly specified (Fawkes, Hu, Evans & Sejdinovic, 2024). DR-DETT control reweighting corrected. effect---treated control counterfactual reweighting controls inverted treatment odds (1 - e) / e; now uses correct treatment odds e / (1 - e) augmented outcome-model correction control arm. seed= now makes permutation tests reproducible. C++ permutation routines drew Armadillo’s internal RNG, ignores set.seed(); now draw R’s RNG, fixed seed reproduces null distribution p-value hsic_test(), mmd_test(), bd_hsic_test(), dr_date_test(), dr_dett_test() permutation-based sensitivity paths exactly. hierarchical_test() longer silently swallows within-cluster failures. Failed within-cluster sub-tests counted surfaced via warning (error every eligible cluster fails). Clusters small chosen sub-test (DR sub-tests need least 30 observations) now skipped clear message excluded within-cluster average, rather silently contributing zero. Integer-overflow hardening C++ HSIC normaliser ((double)(n * n) (double)n * n).

what: Conditional-permutation total-order significance (closes P0 #1 remaining work)
title: kernR 0.2.0
text: New arg total_order_test = c("none", "cond_perm"). title: kernR 0.2.0
text: Two complementary downscaling primitives, rooted Park-Muandet-Fukumizu-Sejdinovic / Szabó-Sriperumbudur-Póczos-Gretton family RKHS regression methods. kernel_downscale(coarse, fine, new_coarse, ...): vector-/ vector-kernel-based downscaling via conditional mean embedding (Park, Muandet, Fukumizu, Sejdinovic 2013). Trains CME paired (coarse, fine) data predicts fine-resolution outputs new coarse inputs. Multi-output fine supported. Lambda LOO-CV default. S3: print, .data.frame. Returns n_new x n_train weight matrix demand. dist_regression(bags, y, ...): bag-/ vector-distribution regression (Szabó, Sriperumbudur, Póczos, Gretton 2016). input bag points mapped empirical mean embedding; outer = "linear" (inner-product embeddings) "rbf" (Gaussian embedding-space distance). Variable bag sizes supported. Multivariate y supported. predict() method --bag prediction. S3: print, .data.frame. fit_cme() predict.cme_fit() now exported (previously @keywords internal); remain lower-level building block, kernel_downscale() user-facing wrapper. New vignette kernR-downscaling: covers methods worked ag-systems examples (coarse climate → paddock yield; bag--soil-cores → paddock yield).

title: kernR 0.2.0
text: nystrom_factor(): Nystrom low-rank kernel factorisation (Williams & Seeger, 2001). Returns n x m factor F F %*% t(F) \approx K [kernel_spec()]. O(n m^2) construction, O(n m) storage. Honours DESCRIPTION’s Nystrom claim. rff_features(): Random Fourier Features (Rahimi & Recht, 2007) RBF kernels. Returns n x D feature matrix Phi %*% t(Phi) \approx K. Data-independent random projection. Honours DESCRIPTION’s RFF claim. hsic_test_nystrom(): Drop-accelerated HSIC independence test via low-rank factorisation. method = "nystrom" (default) "rff". O(n m_x m_y) per permutation (vs O(n^2) exact test); verdict-equivalent hsic_test() moderate m. Uses biased HSIC estimator (form factors cleanly low-rank approximations). New vignette kernR-nystrom: correctness check + scaling benchmark + --use guide.

title: kernR 0.2.0
text: Initial development version. Kernel engine: RBF, Matern, linear, polynomial kernels RcppArmadillo backend. Bandwidth selection: Median heuristic (Rcpp), Scott’s rule. Base tests: hsic_test() (independence), mmd_test() (two-sample). bd_hsic_test(): Backdoor-adjusted HSIC causal association testing. dr_date_test(): Doubly robust distributional average treatment effect. dr_dett_test(): Doubly robust distributional effect treated. Hierarchical: hierarchical_test() nested/clustered data within/decomposition. Unified interface: kernel_causal_test() formula syntax y ~ treatment | confounders. Density ratio estimation: Logistic NCE via glm/ranger/xgboost; RuLSIF (kernel-based). Propensity scores: Cross-fitted estimation trimming diagnostics. Diagnostics: assess_overlap(), plot_weights(), effective_sample_size(). Vignettes: Quick start, bd-HSIC tutorial, DR-DATE/DETT tutorial, hierarchical data.
